records.ety: [L. intercedere, intercessum; inter between + cedere to pass: cf. F. intercder. See Cede.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Interceded; p. pr. & vb. n. Interceding.]

quotes: He supposed that a vast period interceded between that origination and the age wherein he lived.

defs: To act between parties with a view to reconcile differences; to make intercession; to beg or plead in behalf of another; to mediate; -- usually followed by with and for or on behalf of; as, I will intercede with him for you.
quotes: I to the lords will intercede, not doubting Their favorable ear.

defs: To be, to come, or to pass, between; to separate.
